title: Today's AI Brief: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI
summary: Pope Leo XIV’s first encyclical on AI, thermal cameras and AI for whale tracking
imageAlt: Image from source article for What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI
imageCaption: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI
imageSourceTitle: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got a few interesting stories to share with you. First up, Pope Leo XIV has released his first encyclical, titled 'Magnifica Humanitas,' which touches on the power of AI. The Pope notes that algorithms are increasingly deciding what we see, read, and even influencing collective choices. It's an interesting perspective on the role of AI in our lives.

According to Wired, the encyclical is a call to consider the impact of AI on humanity. I find it fascinating that the Pope is weighing in on this topic. It shows just how far-reaching the implications of AI have become. The article highlights how algorithms are not just influencing what we see online, but also filtering what we read and even governing work and information. It's a reminder that AI is not just a tech issue, but a societal one.

In another story, IEEE Spectrum reports that thermal cameras and AI are being used to help ships steer clear of gray whales in San Francisco Bay. This is a great example of AI being used for safety and conservation. By tracking whale movements, ships can avoid collisions, which is a win-win for both humans and whales. It's heartening to see AI being used in practical ways to make a positive impact.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
